skill.name: defi-protocol-templates
skill.description: Create Solidity templates for staking, AMM, governance token, lending, flash loan, and yield farming protocols.
skill.summary: Generate starter DeFi smart contract templates and implementation guidance.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer hits for dynamic require, Ruby backticks, C2 terms, weak crypto, and reconnaissance are false positives from Solidity syntax, Markdown fences, and DeFi terminology. No prompt injection, network exfiltration, filesystem access, or executable helper scripts were found in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is medium because simplified smart contract examples are described as production-ready and could be unsafe if copied into live financial systems.

medium_findings.title: Simplified DeFi Templates Presented as Production Ready
medium_findings.description: The skill describes the examples as production-ready while the single file contains compact Solidity templates for staking, AMMs, governance, and flash loans. These examples omit several safeguards expected before live financial deployment, so the marketplace should present them as starter templates that require review, tests, and audits.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly frames the templates as production-ready, and the later best-practices section still tells users to audit before launch. This is a content-safety concern, not evidence of malicious code.

medium_findings.title: Starter Smart Contracts Lack Key Deployment Safeguards
medium_findings.description: The AMM, governance, and flash loan examples are educational templates that omit controls such as slippage protection, timelock execution details, complete receiver validation, and broader invariant testing guidance. These gaps can create real loss risk if users deploy the snippets without hardening.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited Solidity examples are intentionally compact and omit production hardening paths. The evidence supports a medium-risk warning because the skill is about high-value financial contracts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Dynamic Require Detections Are Solidity Guards
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged dynamic require patterns, but the cited lines are Solidity require statements used for runtime validation. They are not Node.js require calls, do not load modules, and do not execute host code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited dynamic require location is inside a Solidity code block and uses Solidity's require keyword. No JavaScript module loading is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Detections Are Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are Markdown fences around Solidity examples. They are documentation delimiters, not executable shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks appear only as fenced code block markers in a Markdown document. The skill contains no Ruby, shell script, or command invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C2 and Weak Crypto Terms Are DeFi Vocabulary
low_findings.description: The high-severity keyword findings are triggered by normal Solidity and DeFi terms such as contract imports, token interfaces, receiver callbacks, proposal descriptions, and timelocks. No command-and-control behavior, cryptographic downgrade, or system reconnaissance intent is visible.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms are embedded in Solidity examples and DeFi best-practice text. No network endpoints, encoded payloads, persistence logic, or host reconnaissance commands are present.

content.value_statement: DeFi teams need repeatable starting points for common smart contract protocols. This skill provides Solidity templates and implementation guidance for staking, AMMs, governance, lending, and flash loans.
actual_capabilities: Drafts Solidity starter contracts for staking reward systems.
Provides AMM pool logic with liquidity, swap, mint, and burn flows.
Outlines governance token and proposal voting structures.
Includes a flash loan provider and receiver example.
Summarizes DeFi patterns such as TWAP, timelocks, multisig, and vesting.
limitations: Templates require professional audit before mainnet deployment.
Examples do not include full test suites or formal verification.
Protocol economics must be adapted for each token and market.
The skill does not deploy contracts or manage private keys.

use_cases.title: Prototype a staking product
use_cases.description: Create a staking contract outline with rewards, withdrawals, and claim behavior for early technical review.

use_cases.title: Plan a DeFi architecture
use_cases.description: Compare AMM, governance, lending, and flash loan components before selecting a protocol design.

use_cases.title: Prepare audit discussions
use_cases.description: Use the templates to identify expected controls, test areas, and questions for external auditors.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a DeFi template
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right DeFi template for a protocol that needs [staking, swaps, lending, governance, or flash loans]. Explain the main components and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt a staking design
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a staking reward design for [token name] with [reward schedule], [lockup rules], and [admin controls]. Include security checks I should add.

prompt_templates.title: Review an AMM plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my AMM requirements for [asset pair], [fee model], and [oracle needs]. Identify missing protections before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Harden a protocol template
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn the [selected DeFi template] into a production-readiness checklist. Include testing, access control, upgrade, monitoring, and audit requirements.

output_examples.input: Design a staking protocol for a governance token.
output: A staking architecture with deposit, withdraw, claim, and emergency controls.
A reward accounting model with update points and user balances.
A security checklist covering reentrancy, token transfer safety, and reward funding.

output_examples.input: Review the AMM template before I build from it.
output: A list of required protections for slippage, deadlines, fee accounting, and reserve updates.
A test plan for liquidity operations, swaps, edge cases, and invariant checks.
A warning that the template is a starting point and not deployable without hardening.

output_examples.input: Prepare a governance contract implementation plan.
output: A proposal flow covering threshold checks, voting period, execution, and event logging.
Recommendations for timelocks, quorum rules, delegated votes, and admin constraints.
Questions to resolve before audit and mainnet deployment.

best_practices: Treat every generated contract as a draft until tested, reviewed, and audited.
Use established libraries for token handling, access control, pausing, and reentrancy protection.
Define protocol economics, threat models, and emergency procedures before deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy template snippets directly to mainnet.
Do not omit tests for edge cases, invariants, and adversarial token behavior.
Do not rely on a single admin key for critical protocol controls.

faq.question: Can this skill create deployable DeFi contracts?
faq.answer: It can create starter templates and guidance. You still need testing, threat modeling, and an independent audit before deployment.

faq.question: Which protocols does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers staking, AMMs, governance tokens, lending concepts, flash loans, and related DeFi patterns.

faq.question: Does it manage wallets or private keys?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides code guidance only and does not manage deployments, wallets, or private keys.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it include tests?
faq.answer: The source file does not include a full test suite. Ask for a protocol-specific test plan before implementation.

faq.question: Is this suitable for regulated financial products?
faq.answer: Use it only as technical reference material. Legal, compliance, and security reviews are required for regulated products.
